reviews.review_description: Video boff

Many people expect this to be a great VR game. But it's just a platform to download 3D 180° videos.
Once you adjust your expectations however, it's really great. I've enjoyed all first 5 seasons. And the hidden vids. Watching people create things and present their creations. Talk about the process of making things. Etc. Thanks you to the devs and all that participated in creating the content. Loved it!

I’m a little biased because me and my Dad would watch Mythbusters all the time and I enjoy the “Tested” channel on YouTube.  I really like what the developer did with certain aspects like the cardboard aesthetic, the physics, being able to use the pencil to draw, throwing various objects off the desk and the content is interesting.  I would love to see more but I love what they have done.

I love watching tested on YouTube so I picked this up, the videos are diverse and well done except they are a bit dated and like most older 180 3d videos they warp a bit when moving your head but the content is great.   Note that it isn't a game you have 7 videos plus the intro you download and watch, just loved seeing a 3d view of the cave.
It's free, It's awesome, and I have no regret, but it should save your progress on a video
SO I THOUGHT WE COULD BULID AND STUFF AND WHEN I PLAYED I GOT STUCK WATCHING VIDEOS INSTEAD OF PLAYING A REAL GAME.
I would want to build stuff in this it would be a better game if you could build stuff but instead you watch videos
I thought you were going to do sandbox stuff but no you watch dumb videos this is so lame 0/10 recommendation do not get this game

When I got this off the Quest Store, I thought it would be a fun game where I got to build things with Adam Savage guiding me. Instead, I got eye strain. This is essentially a VR video player that does not work well at all. It’s educational seeing what people can create, but definitely heavy on the eyes and looks terrible. Definitely not what anyone is expecting when they see “Adam Savage VR”. 

2/10, would not Adam Savage again.

This is not really VR - it's essentially a short 3D film series, best appreciated looking straight ahead. If you move side to side or even look around, the 3D effect "breaks" and it's really disorienting. I realize it's really difficult to record 360 video in 3D, and it shows - this is essentially a 3D video that tries to fake immersion..... No comment on the content, as I couldn't watch it long enough to judge.

I enjoy watching the adam savages tested channel on YouTube so this was an absolute joy to watch. Standing at the other side of the work bench was amazing and thrilling to have a look around the cave which I've seen so many times on his channel 10/10 grinning the first time I met Adam Savage In VR
This is not what I was expecting
Some guy wrote a essay calling people that don't like this stupid. Well call me stupid this app is a waste of time. Its free yes but waste of time.
People u dint understand why you say its just waste its free and if u dont wanna this game nust delete it

The stuff we dont see when it comes to movies and shows from stop prop animation to making puppets, intuitive videos showing the behind the scenes look at all that magic clever sub interactive menus let u display and play around on the work desk to watch in any order.

If your into special features and watch them after a film to know how things are made then this is right down your street lots of good content ...and its free

reviews.review_title: Really cool look into Adam Savages cave
reviews.review_description:
I love this app, it’s great to feel like you’re actually in the cave with Adam as he talks about things he hasn’t shown on the channel before.  Adam is as engaging as ever, and really knows how to spark curiosity and creativity through showing off his creations and process.

The only downside to me is that the videos have a mildly headache inducing flicker on the Quest 2, which I think is from the fluorescent lights in the cave.  Other than that everything is crisp and the content is great!

I have to confess I was a little underwhelmed and disappointed by the "VR" in this title which otherwise was interesting subject matter I'm sure for many. The VR pov for the observer is stationary, so if you try to look down or side to side the entire picture and everything in it shifts with you in each of the individual episodes. So you have to keep your head still and just watch as if you are watching in 2D. There was alot of wide angle distortion as well if you didn't look straight ahead, which in my view defeated the purpose of recording in a real workshop that you really couldn't look around in. The content was informative. The video format used not enjoyable imo.
